pattern: At the first descent you have exactly two repair choices, and a single greedy decision tells you whether one removal suffices.
hints: A strictly increasing array has at most zero violations of nums[i-1] < nums[i].
If you find more than one violation you cannot fix it with a single removal.
At the first violation, try removing either the earlier or the later element and check whether the rest stays strictly increasing.

edge_inputs.description: remove a spike in the middle
all equal needs more than one removal
removing earlier element fails but later succeeds
removing later element fails but earlier succeeds

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    cur = 1
    a = []
    for _ in range(n):
        cur += rng.randrange(0, 3)
        a.append(cur)
    if rng.random() < 0.7:
        idx = rng.randrange(0, n)
        a[idx] = rng.randrange(1, 1001)
    return [a]
